articles.title: A Boxing Club Gets a Surprise Gift
articles.summary: A boxing club in Wales was very surprised when a famous boxer named Jake Paul sent them help. The club, called Cwmavon Hornets, lost its head coach Nigel Davies, who was like a father to many boxers. Jake Paul heard about the club from a former boxer who works for him. He decided to donate 16 pairs of boxing gloves to support the club. The club members were excited and couldn't believe Jake Paul knew about their small club. This story shows how kindness can bring joy to people during sad times.

articles.title: A Famous Model and Actress from Dallas Has Passed Away
articles.summary: Annabel Schofield was a model and actress who became famous in the 1980s. She was born in Wales and moved to London at age 16 to start modeling. She worked with big fashion brands like Versace and starred in a popular jeans commercial. Later, she acted in the TV show Dallas and lived in Los Angeles. Sadly, she died at age 62 from cancer in February. She had been sick for a while and talked about her illness to get help with treatment costs. People remember her as beautiful, funny, and kind. Her modeling agency said she was very important to their success. She also wrote a book and worked as a producer. Her story shows how she achieved her dreams but faced a tough illness.

articles.title: Actor's Words Upset Ballet and Opera Fans
articles.summary: Timothée Chalamet, a famous actor, said something that made many people in ballet and opera upset. He talked about keeping art forms alive and mentioned that no one cares about ballet or opera anymore. He quickly said he respects the people who do these arts. But his words caused a big reaction. Stars from stage shows, opera houses, and ballet companies criticized him. Some people, like Jamie Lee Curtis, asked why artists would say bad things about other artists. Others, like Deepa Johnny, said artists should work together to support all art. The ballet community said ballet is very much alive and many people enjoy it. This shows that words can hurt, and it's important to be kind to everyone's art.

articles.title: Henry Patten's Scary Trip Home from Dubai
articles.summary: Henry Patten is a tennis player from Britain who was in Dubai for a tennis tournament. While he was there, there was a conflict in the Middle East, and Iran attacked the United Arab Emirates with missiles. This made it hard for Henry and other people to go home. He saw a drone hit a building near where he was staying. Henry had to stay in Dubai longer than planned and tried to drive to Oman, but it didn't work. Finally, he took a flight back to London, but he was very scared because he doesn't like flying. On the plane, he felt nervous, but he made it home safely. This experience made him decide not to play in another tennis tournament in Indian Wells.

articles.title: Dave's Big Concert in London
articles.summary: Dave, a famous British musician, performed a special concert at the London O2 Arena to celebrate 10 years in music. He played songs from his new album and old hits, and fans sang along and recorded videos. Dave thanked his fans and said he felt happy to be home in London. He talked to young fans, telling them they are powerful and have bright futures. The show had a band and a stage that let Dave get close to the audience, making it feel personal. It was a fun night celebrating his music journey.

articles.title: Oil Prices Go Up After Qatar Warning
articles.summary: Oil prices have jumped to their highest in over two years because Qatar's energy minister warned that all oil and gas production in the Gulf might stop soon due to conflict in the Middle East. This region is important for global energy supplies. When oil prices rise, it affects many things like the cost of filling up cars, heating homes, and buying food. In the UK, petrol and diesel prices have already gone up. Experts say if the conflict continues, it could hurt the world's economy by making energy more expensive and causing shortages. This situation is being watched closely to see if it becomes a bigger crisis.
